meta.description: Ranks papers by evidence family, methodological quality tier, validation depth, and claim discipline; assigns anchor, context-setting, mechanistic support, or caution citation roles. Polished: frontmatter normalized to canonical schema; reference module integration corrected to actual file names; p-value proxy check added to Step 3; Input Validation section added.

scientific_integrity.detail: Hard Rules 11-14 prohibit fabricating references, PMIDs, DOIs, validation claims, sample sizes, and effect estimates; Section J verification notes requirement enforced.

practice_boundaries.detail: Explicitly prohibits turning evidence ranking into clinical advice or treatment recommendation; citation priority framing correctly scoped to manuscript support use.

methodological_ground.detail: Four-dimension ranking framework (evidence family, methodological quality, validation depth, claim discipline) is methodologically sound; Hard Rule 1 correctly separates design label from true evidence value.

code_usability.detail: Mode A evidence appraisal skill; no code generated.

functional_suitability.note: Four-dimension ranking framework is comprehensive; 5 citation roles provide actionable output; minor gap: frontmatter contains non-standard fields (category, subcategory, tags, version) beyond the required name/description/license/skill-author standard.

reliability.note: Proceeds with available materials when input is incomplete — appropriate fallback behavior; 3 of 5 referenced modules in SKILL.md reference integration section do not exist in the references/ directory (study-design-identification.md, result-reliability-principles.md, validation-chain-rules.md); no structured minimum-input clarification protocol.

performance_context.note: 249-line SKILL.md well-proportioned for task scope; directory contains 9 reference files but only 2 match SKILL.md's listed module names (claim-discipline-rules.md, literature-integrity-rules.md) — orphaned/misnamed file overhead.

agent_usability.note: 8-step execution sequence is clear; citation role framework is actionable; reference integration section lists non-existent file names which may confuse agents attempting module lookup; description is too brief for reliable triggering.

human_usability.note: Sample triggers are natural and specific; five citation roles are clearly named and easy to understand; description brevity limits discoverability.

security.note: No credentials or sensitive data handling; no injection vectors; 17 hard rules provide comprehensive anti-fabrication and anti-prestige-ranking posture.

maintainability.note: Non-standard frontmatter fields reduce schema compliance; major file mismatch: 3 module names in SKILL.md reference integration do not correspond to files in directory; 7 files in directory not referenced in SKILL.md — significant maintenance confusion for skill updates.

agent_specific.note: Five citation roles (anchor/high-value/context/mechanistic/caution) provide good citation-use differentiation; description too brief for precise triggering; no composability hooks to manuscript writing or systematic review skills; escape hatch for scope violations (clinical decision requests) is present.

inputs.label: Rank a meta-analysis, RCT, cohort, and mechanism paper on the same clinical question
inputs.note: All four dimensions assessed separately; meta-analysis not auto-ranked #1; citation roles assigned with explicit reasoning; uncertainties section present.

assertions.text: Evidence family, methodological quality, validation depth, and claim discipline assessed separately for each paper
assertions.note: All four dimensions addressed independently in Sections B-E.

assertions.text: Meta-analysis not automatically ranked #1 without checking execution quality (Hard Rule 4)
assertions.note: Meta-analysis methodological quality (heterogeneity, publication bias, search comprehensiveness) evaluated before ranking.

assertions.text: Citation role assigned to each paper using the five-role taxonomy (anchor/high-value/context/mechanistic/caution)
assertions.note: Section G citation role assignments present for all four papers.

assertions.text: Ranking reasoning explicit in Section H — not just an ordered list without justification
assertions.note: Key factors driving the ranking order explained.

assertions.text: Ranking uncertainties and caveats present in Section I
assertions.note: Uncertainty sources identified; incomplete access or missing statistical detail noted where applicable.

inputs.label: Rank mixed papers serving different evidence roles — not directly comparable
inputs.note: Non-comparable papers identified rather than forced into single ladder; different evidence roles explained; clinical vs mechanistic separation maintained; journal prestige not used as criterion.

assertions.text: Papers serving different evidence roles identified as non-comparable rather than forced into a single ranking ladder (Hard Rules 15-16)
assertions.note: Comparison logic explained; role-differentiated framing used instead of simplistic rank order.

assertions.text: Different evidence roles explained with comparison logic in Section H
assertions.note: Why different roles exist and how they relate to each other made explicit.

assertions.text: Clinical evidence separated from mechanistic evidence for citation purpose (Hard Rule 8)
assertions.note: Mechanistic papers assigned mechanistic support citation role; not conflated with clinical anchor.

assertions.text: Journal prestige not used as ranking criterion (Hard Rule 2)
assertions.note: No mention of journal impact factor or citation count as ranking basis.

assertions.text: No fabricated bibliographic details for any paper
assertions.note: Hard Rules 11-14 enforced; unverifiable details labeled as unverified.

inputs.label: Rank papers where higher-tier design has weak execution — RCT below well-executed cohort
inputs.note: Poorly executed RCT correctly ranked below well-executed cohort; overclaim pattern identified; caution citation applied. One instance of statistical significance used as partial proxy for methodological quality.

assertions.text: Poorly executed RCT ranked below well-executed cohort with explicit justification
assertions.note: Hard Rule 4 enforced; execution quality overrides nominal design tier.

assertions.text: Execution quality dimensions assessed (sampling, bias control, sample size, statistical discipline)
assertions.note: Step 3 dimensions applied to both papers.

assertions.text: Overclaim pattern identified in the higher-tier but lower-quality paper
assertions.note: Claim discipline review (Section E) catches conclusion overreach.

assertions.text: Caution citation role correctly applied to overclaiming paper
assertions.note: Section G correctly assigns caution citation despite the RCT design label.

assertions.text: Statistical significance not equated with methodological reliability (Hard Rule 3)
assertions.note: One instance where p < 0.05 result cited as partial evidence of methodological strength — conflates statistical significance with methodological reliability.

inputs.label: Rank papers where meta-analysis has high I² heterogeneity not reported by authors
inputs.note: Heterogeneity identified as quality limitation; meta-analysis not auto-ranked above primary studies; claim discipline appropriately downgraded; no fabricated I² statistics.

assertions.text: Heterogeneity acknowledged as a key quality limitation for the meta-analysis
assertions.note: Unreported high heterogeneity flagged as methodological weakness in Section C.

assertions.text: Meta-analysis not automatically placed above all primary studies given heterogeneity concerns (Hard Rule 4)
assertions.note: Execution quality correctly overrides nominal design hierarchy.

assertions.text: Claim discipline reviewed — pooled estimate uncertainty flagged due to unstated heterogeneity
assertions.note: Section E identifies overclaiming risk when pooled estimate is presented without heterogeneity disclosure.

assertions.text: Citation role appropriately downgraded from anchor to high-value support or context-setting given heterogeneity
assertions.note: Section G citation role reflects methodological quality limitation.

assertions.text: No fabricated heterogeneity statistics (I² values, Cochran's Q) introduced
assertions.note: Hard Rules 11-14 enforced; missing statistical details labeled as not reported.

inputs.label: Rank 7 mixed-family papers (meta-analysis, RCT, cohort, case-control, mechanism, omics, review) for a manuscript
inputs.note: Evidence families correctly identified for all 7; non-comparable roles handled; clinical vs mechanistic maintained. Minor: citation role assignments for 7 papers show some grouping without adequate per-paper differentiation.

assertions.text: Evidence family correctly identified for all 7 papers including omics discovery and computational evidence families
assertions.note: Hard Rule 7 enforced; all evidence families distinguished.

assertions.text: Non-comparable papers across 7 designs not forced into a misleading single-number ranking (Hard Rule 15)
assertions.note: Comparison logic explains why different papers serve different evidentiary roles.

assertions.text: Each of the 7 papers receives an individual citation role with per-paper reasoning
assertions.note: 2-3 papers with similar designs grouped into shared citation role assessment without individual-paper reasoning — violates Hard Rule 17 (ranking must be transparent in reasoning).

assertions.text: Clinical evidence family separated from mechanistic and omics families for citation priority
assertions.note: Hard Rule 7 maintained throughout 7-paper comparison.

assertions.text: No fabricated details for any of the 7 papers
assertions.note: Hard Rules 11-14 enforced across all papers.

inputs.label: Request to rank papers to support a clinical treatment decision for a specific patient
inputs.note: Clinical treatment decision correctly identified as beyond citation-priority scope; evidence ranking provided for manuscript/research use; no treatment recommendation generated.

assertions.text: Request for clinical treatment decision support identified as beyond the skill's citation-priority scope
assertions.note: Skill correctly distinguishes citation priority ranking from clinical decision evidence appraisal.

assertions.text: Evidence ranking for manuscript/research purpose provided without clinical treatment recommendation
assertions.note: Output stays within citation priority scope; clinical guidance explicitly excluded.

assertions.text: User directed to clinical guideline resources or treating clinician for patient-specific decision support
assertions.note: Appropriate redirect for the out-of-scope clinical decision component.

assertions.text: No clinical recommendation, treatment dosing, or prescribing guidance produced
assertions.note: Hard prohibition enforced: 'Do not act as a treatment recommendation engine.'

assertions.text: Citation priority and clinical evidence for treatment correctly distinguished as different constructs
assertions.note: The distinction between 'best citation for a claim' and 'best evidence for clinical decision' explicitly stated.

inputs.label: Rank papers provided as titles only — no abstract, methods, or results available
inputs.note: Proceeds with available material per input validation policy; limitations labeled. Minor: methodological quality claims from titles alone not consistently labeled as provisional throughout Sections C-E.

assertions.text: Methodological quality claims based on titles alone labeled as provisional [TITLE ONLY — METHODOLOGY NOT VERIFIABLE] throughout Sections C-E
assertions.note: Some methodology inferences from titles stated without consistent provisional labeling — violates Hard Rule 13 (label what cannot be verified from provided material).

assertions.text: Major uncertainty sources labeled explicitly per input validation policy
assertions.note: Section I uncertainty: title-only input clearly identified as severely limiting methodological quality assessment.

assertions.text: Skill proceeds with available materials and produces best-available ranking with uncertainty labels
assertions.note: Input validation policy correctly applied: ranking attempted with explicit limitation disclosure.

assertions.text: No fabricated methodological details (sample size, cohort definition, statistical methods) invented from title alone
assertions.note: Hard Rules 11-14 enforced; no invented study details.

assertions.text: Ranking limitations acknowledged in Section I with note that full assessment requires abstract and methods access
assertions.note: Section I correctly indicates what additional information would upgrade the ranking confidence.

key_strengths: Four-dimension ranking framework (evidence family, methodological quality, validation depth, claim discipline) prevents design-label-to-rank conflation — a common appraisal error
Five citation roles (anchor, high-value support, context-setting, mechanistic support, caution) give manuscript authors actionable guidance beyond a generic quality score
17 hard rules explicitly address the most common evidence appraisal errors including prestige ranking, statistical significance conflation, and validation overclaiming
Design-role separation rule (Hard Rules 15-16) prevents forcing non-comparable papers into a misleading single-number ranking

recommendations.title: Reference module integration lists 3 files that do not exist in references/ directory
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md reference integration section references study-design-identification.md, result-reliability-principles.md, and validation-chain-rules.md — none of these files exist in the references/ directory. 7 files in the directory (citation-priority-rules.md, cross-design-ranking-framework.md, evidence-family-taxonomy.md, methodological-quality-audit-rules.md, validation-depth-rules.md, workflow-step-template.md, output-section-guidance.md) are not referenced in SKILL.md.
recommendations.root_cause: Reference module names in SKILL.md were not synchronized with the actual files present in the references/ directory — likely a renaming operation left the integration section stale.
recommendations.fix: Update the Reference Module Integration section to reference the actual files in the directory: evidence-family-taxonomy.md (Step 2), methodological-quality-audit-rules.md (Step 3), validation-depth-rules.md (Step 4), claim-discipline-rules.md (Step 5), citation-priority-rules.md (Step 6), cross-design-ranking-framework.md (Steps 6-7), literature-integrity-rules.md (Section J), output-section-guidance.md, workflow-step-template.md.

recommendations.title: Non-standard frontmatter fields violate SKILL.md schema
recommendations.problem: The frontmatter contains non-standard fields: category, subcategory, tags, and version. These are not part of the required skill schema (name, description, license, skill-author) and may cause parser errors or inconsistent behavior in skill loading systems.
recommendations.root_cause: Frontmatter extended with additional metadata fields beyond the required schema.
recommendations.fix: Remove category, subcategory, tags, and version from frontmatter. If version tracking is needed, move to a references/metadata.md file or a comment in the SKILL.md body.

recommendations.title: Statistical significance used as partial proxy for methodological quality
recommendations.problem: In one evaluation instance, a statistically significant result was cited as partial evidence of methodological strength, conflating statistical significance with methodological reliability.
recommendations.root_cause: Hard Rule 3 prohibits this conflation but is not reinforced in the Step 3 execution instructions where methodological quality is assessed.
recommendations.fix: Add an explicit check in Step 3 (Methodological Quality): 'Do not use statistical significance or reported effect size magnitude as evidence of good methods — assess sampling logic, bias control, and study design directly and independently of reported p-values.'

recommendations.title: Description too brief to differentiate from generic citation management or literature review tools
recommendations.problem: The single-sentence description does not include key trigger phrases that would distinguish this skill from general literature tools.
recommendations.root_cause: Description optimized for accuracy but not for triggering precision.
recommendations.fix: Expand description to include: 'ranks papers by evidence family, methodological quality tier, validation depth, and claim discipline; assigns anchor, context-setting, mechanistic support, or caution citation roles; prevents prestige-based or design-label-based ranking errors'.
